title: Eschweilera cordata
intro: Eschweilera cordata, also known as Cordia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eschweilera cordata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory and skin problems.
